texto: The modern Westerner, persuaded that he has a right to "think for himself" and imagining that he exercises this right, is unwilling to acknowledge that his every thought has been shaped by cultural and historical influences and that his opinions fit, like pieces of jigsaw puzzle, into a pattern which has nothing random about it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charles Le Gai Eaton: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “The modern Westerner, persuaded that he has a right to "think for himself" and imagining that he …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, thinking, right pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: The modern Westerner, persuaded that he has a right to "think for himself" and imagining that he exercises this right, is unwilling to acknowledge that his every thought has been shaped by cultural and historical influences and that his opinions fit, like pieces of jigsaw puzzle, into a pattern which has nothing random about it.

texto: Man is either Viceroy or else he is an animal that claims special rights by virtue of its cunning and the devouring efficiency of teeth sharpened by technological instruments... But if he is Viceroy, then all decay and trouble in the created world that surrounds him is in some measure to be laid to his account
explicacao: Citação de Charles Le Gai Eaton sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Man is either Viceroy or else he is an animal that claims special rights by virtue of its cunning…” articula reflexao, accountability, responsibility em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como responsibility.
